item.title: Are exploits free?
item.author: 152334H
item.description: For various reasons, I had to develop a kernel n-day exploit last week.

item.title: Things worth doing
item.author: 152334H
item.description: Most of my life has been dedicated towards intellectual excellence, but continuing to do so would be a mistake.

item.title: Truncated Thoughts on 2025
item.author: 152334H
item.description: It has been, overall, a pretty shit year.

item.title: Time To Think
item.author: 152334H
item.description: I don’t remember what it’s like to think with more than 60 seconds of context.

item.title: Calculating the Cost of a Google Deepmind Paper
item.author: 152334H
item.description:
Recently, GDM released a great paper titled, Scaling Exponents Across Parameterizations and Optimizers, in which they conduct over 10,000 LLM training runs to obtain optimal hyperparameters under different regimes.

After reading it (it was great), I wanted to test my understanding of the paper by tallying up all experiments conducted within, calculating the total compute cost it would take to replicate the paper.

item.title: DeepSeek Core Readings 0 - Coder
item.author: 152334H
item.description:
Paper summary: 1.3B to 33B LLMs on 1/2T code tokens (87 langs) w/ FiM and 16K seqlen. Strong effort in constructing pretraining data from Github from scratch, with repository-level samples. Evals beat OSS code models solidly + GPT-3.5 a bit; Coder-7B > CodeLlama-33B often.

They don’t spend much effort on Instruction tuning. They commit a continued pretrain of DeepSeek LLM -> Coder: I believe it underperforms; they don’t.

item.title: DeepSeek Core Readings 1 - LLM
item.author: 152334H
item.description: Paper summary: LLaMA-like 7B/67B pretrain (Base) + SFT&DPO (Chat). 2T tokens with strong CN/EN mix, >1mil SFT examples. Well-executed exploration of scaling laws. Good details about evals and safety. Not much described about their actual data.

item.title: Basic tips for remaining conscious
item.author: 152334H
item.description: I don’t think very often. These are some of the things I’ve tried in the past to rectify that.

item.title: Rough thoughts on Mixtral vs Open Source
item.author: 152334H
item.description:
Here’s a thesis (hypothesis, predicate, etc) to chew on:

The mixture-of-experts paradigm is fundamentally a hinderance to open source development, and mixtral-8x5B+2B will be summarily supplanted by a dense model like llama3/mistral-70b/yi/qwen/… in the near future.
